lesson.title: CAPS.Mathematics.Gr11.ALG.1: Simplify expressions involving surds. Apply laws of expon...
lesson.explanation: In Grade 10, you master factorisation — the reverse of expanding. Methods: (1) Common factor: take out the greatest common factor. (2) Grouping: group terms in pairs and factor each pair. (3) Trinomials: find two numbers that multiply to ac and add to b in ax² + bx + c. (4) Difference of squares: a² - b² = (a+b)(a-b). (5) Sum/difference of cubes: a³ + b³ = (a+b)(a²-ab+b²).

assessment.title: Assessment: Simplify expressions involving surds. Apply laws of exponents to real-number exponents. Add, subtract, multiply, divide surds

flashcards.front: What is the key idea in Surds and Simplification?
flashcards.back: A surd is an irrational number expressed as a root, like √2 or ∛5
